reasoning: The coin has genuine charm — the star cutout center gives personality and the golden pixel-art palette reads as a classic game reward item. At 4x on dark bg it pops well and the circular-gear silhouette is legible. However: (1) at 1x the sprite collapses to an indistinct orange blob with no readable shape, failing the sticker test; (2) on the light/cream background scattered stray pixels (blue/teal noise) are visible around the perimeter indicating dirty edges; (3) the gear-ray border adds visual interest at 4x but overcrowds the silhouette — it reads as 'spiky blob' rather than 'coin' at small scale; (4) orange-on-cream contrast is noticeably weaker than orange-on-dark, making light-bg readability marginal. Charming but not yet clean or small-scale-safe.
